seoTitle: How to Export a Power BI Report to PDF
metaDescription: Export your Power BI report to PDF in a few quick steps, perfect for sharing polished, static versions with stakeholders or by email.
intro: Sometimes stakeholders just need a static, shareable version of your report rather than access to the live Power BI file, and PDF export is the easiest way to deliver that. This guide covers the steps to export a report to PDF and customize the output. It's a quick skill that saves time when you need to send reports by email or for formal documentation.

steps.heading: Access the report you want to export
steps.body: Open the specific Power BI report you plan to convert to PDF before starting the export process.

steps.heading: Choose the export option
steps.body: Select the export feature within Power BI and choose PDF as your output format.

steps.heading: Customize the PDF settings
steps.body: Adjust any available settings, such as page layout or which pages to include, so the PDF matches what you want to share.

steps.heading: Finalize the export
steps.body: Complete the export process and save the generated PDF file to your device.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for sharing
steps.body: Review the exported PDF before sending it out to confirm the visuals and data appear as expected in the static format.

faq.question: Will my Power BI report look exactly the same in PDF form?
faq.answer: It's generally close, though some interactive elements won't function since a PDF is a static, non-interactive format.

faq.question: Can I export just specific pages of a report to PDF?
faq.answer: Depending on the export options available, you can often choose which pages are included rather than exporting the entire report.

faq.question: Is PDF export available to all report viewers?
faq.answer: Export availability can depend on permissions set by the report owner, so not every viewer may have access to this option.

faq.question: Can I automate PDF exports on a schedule?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI supports scheduling exports and distribution in some workflows, which is useful for regular reporting needs.

faq.question: Why would I export to PDF instead of just sharing the live report?
faq.answer: PDFs are useful for offline sharing, email attachments, and formal documentation where a fixed snapshot is more appropriate than live access.
